response.justification: The candidate is fundamentally a web and TypeScript/Node.js developer with a broad but shallow systems background. C++ usage is limited to a single role at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project; no Rust experience is mentioned or implied. While GISCollective and Green Map demonstrate genuine geospatial interest, the work centers on data collection and visualization rather than routing tile optimization, pipeline latency, or navigation guidance systems. Recent years show a clear trajectory deeper into web product engineering, AI tooling, and developer infrastructure — not toward the systems-level, performance-critical C++ work this role demands.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS domain experience through GISCollective and Green Map, including an event-driven geo-data platform
Distributed microservices background at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency DLang applications
Linux familiarity and broad multi-language exposure including some historical C/C++ and systems-adjacent work
concerns: C++ experience is minimal, last used substantively in 2014–2015; no Rust experience at all — the two primary required languages are effectively absent
No routing pipeline, map tiling, navigation guidance, or traffic-signal integration experience of any kind
Current and recent work (2020–present) is entirely TypeScript/Node.js web and AI tooling, moving further from systems programming, not toward it
